The issue with the lookers as they are right now, so you know, all the tickets that are there in linear, right, for all the looker issues, the issue with all those dashboards is that ....
Data is incorrect because what has happened is I think the way those Lookers have been designed, they are pulling data from Airtable.
Basically, every week we're publishing new URLs, right?
So what it's doing is it's trying to automatically pull in the new URLs published from Airtable.
It pulls it into a Google Sheet, and then it basically joins the Google Sheet with Search Console.
So in Looker, they call that a blend, basically, but it's actually a join, right?
And I think they're doing like a full outer join on it, and we're trying to pull in data.
So we're joining on the URL itself, which you might know is a very inefficient join because you want a numeric sheet to join ideally.
But anyway, what happens is it's trying to join on this, and the data that is showing up in the Looker report is completely incorrect because it's introducing some...
sort of sampling over there, right?
So the numbers cannot be trusted and we cannot show that to the clients.
So for example, the traffic might be say 1000, but it's showing 100.
So obviously we can't show that.
So my solution was to remove that automation from Airtable and basically manually create a case statement.
It's a simple SQL case statement in the backend, I'll show you all this, but create a custom field in the backend with all the URLs and each week I manually update the URL list.
So I already have a big URL list.
I just append the new published URLs to that big list.
And that data just gets used in the looker.
So there's no automation, but there is that manual URL updating.
So that gets me the correct data.
The problem is that a lot of MEs do not want to do that manual process.
I think it's very simple.
But they don't want to do it for whatever reason.
